info.name: TIP CM Retention – Categories & Sub-Categories
info.description:
FDIC Treasury Integrated Platform – Content Manager Retention API (R1 Lean MVP)

Covers all Category (US-1.1–US-1.5, US-1.12) and Sub-Category (US-1.6–US-1.11) transactions.

## Setup
1. Set `baseUrl` to `http://localhost:8080` (or your env URL).
2. In **dev profile** use HTTP Basic: set `adminUser`/`adminPass` (default: `admin`/`admin`).
3. Run the **Category – Happy Path** folder top-to-bottom; IDs are captured automatically via test scripts.
4. Then run the **Sub-Category – Happy Path** folder.
5. Error-scenario folders can be run independently — they carry their own static payloads.

## Variable auto-capture
- `categoryId` — captured from Create Category 201 response
- `secondCategoryId` — captured from Create Second Category (used for Move tests)
- `subCategoryId` — captured from Create Sub-Category 201 response

CONTROLLED // FDIC INTERNAL ONLY

item.name: 01 – Categories Happy Path
item.description: Run top-to-bottom. Each request captures the ID needed by the next.

item.name: US-1.1 Create Second Category (for Move tests) → 201
US-1.12 Get Hierarchy – filter by status=draft → 200
US-1.12 Get Hierarchy – search by name → 200
US-1.3 Edit Active Category – description only → 200
02 – Categories Error Scenarios
item.description: Independent error-path tests. Each uses inline static data – no dependency on happy-path variables.

item.name: 03 – Sub-Categories Happy Path
item.description: Requires secondCategoryId (active) from folder 01. Run top-to-bottom.

body.raw:
{
  "categoryId": "{{secondCategoryId}}",
  "code": "INTERNAL_FINDINGS",
  "name": "Internal Findings",
  "description": "Internal examination findings retained for 7 years.",
  "retentionDurationValue": 7,
  "retentionDurationUnit": "years"
}

item.name: GET Sub-Categories by Category – filter status=draft → 200
US-1.8 Edit Sub-Category name only (no retention change) → 200
US-1.8 Edit Sub-Category retention with reason → 202 (cascade queued)
US-1.12 Hierarchy – verify Sub-Category appears active → 200
US-1.9 Move Sub-Category to different parent → 200

exec: // NOTE: This test moves the sub-cat from secondCategoryId.
// It will only make sense if a separate active target category exists.
// Adjust targetCategoryId in the body to a valid active Category UUID.
console.log('Ensure target categoryId in body is a valid active Category.');

item.name: 04 – Sub-Categories Error Scenarios
item.description: Independent error-path tests for Sub-Category endpoints.

item.name: US-1.8 Edit Sub-Cat – code in body → 422
US-1.11 Delete Sub-Cat that has_ever_held_content=true → 409 (hard block)
US-1.9 Move Sub-Cat to inactive target → 422
05 – US-1.5 Delete Category WITH has_ever_held_content
item.description: Demonstrates the X-Confirmation-Code: DELETE CATEGORY header gate. Run against a Category that has had content classified into its Sub-Categories (has_ever_held_content=true).

item.name: US-1.5 Delete – has_ever_held_content, WRONG header value → 409
